data.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Expected Behavior

When create-dso-app.sh runs check_homebrew_deps() and Homebrew is not installed (command -v brew fails), the installer should automatically run the official Homebrew install script:

    /bin/bash -c \"\$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)\"

then continue with subsequent dependency checks.

#### Actual Behavior

scripts/create-dso-app.sh:233-239 (check_homebrew_deps) currently exits with a manual instruction:

    if ! command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo \"Homebrew is required but not installed.\"
        echo \"Install it from https://brew.sh, then re-run this installer.\"
        exit 1
    fi

The user must abandon the install, manually install Homebrew, then re-run create-dso-app.sh — adding friction to the one-command bootstrap experience the script is designed to provide.

#### Files involved

- scripts/create-dso-app.sh (line 233 — check_homebrew_deps function)
data.title: [create-dso-app.sh]: Homebrew not installed -> installer exits instead of auto-installing Homebrew
